page: Amazon Web Services c8i.8xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 vCPUs mapped to 16 dedicated physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor operating at 3.9 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes a massive 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong compute and memory performance. The instance achieves a top-tier rating in Geekbench single-core testing, image processing, and text processing. Multi-core performance is also strong, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 18364.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 51718.7. Memory bandwidth for large, potentially uncached blocks is strong at 178.37 GB/sec. Cryptographic and compression workloads perform exceptionally well, as evidenced by a strong Passmark CPU encryption score and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 34.25 GB/sec. Additionally, the CPU supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for accelerated matrix operations.

This hardware profile is highly cost-efficient for compute-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. The combination of high-speed DDR5 memory and AMX instruction support enables strong performance in lightweight machine learning tasks, such as LLM prompt processing and text generation for small to medium models. The instance is also well-suited for high-throughput web serving, Redis databases, software compilation, and compute-heavy batch processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs across 16 dedicated physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 64.0 GB of 7 GHz DDR5 memory, a 480 MB L3 cache, and a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps, with no local storage or GPUs. The x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core, image, and text processing performance, alongside strong multi-core capabilities and high memory bandwidth. This instance delivers excellent resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for compute-intensive tasks. It is highly suited for workloads such as high-throughput web serving, Redis operations, software compilation, and small-to-medium scale LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 3.9 GHz processor, delivering top-tier single-core and image processing performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A high-frequency, compute-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores, DDR5 memory, and advanced matrix extensions for accelerated workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 32 vCPUs and 16 dedicated physical Intel Xeon cores at 3.9 GHz
Equipped with 64.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Features a massive 480 MB L3 cache and Intel AMX instruction support
Delivers 15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Top-tier performance in single-core, image, and text processing benchmarks
Optimized for web serving, Redis, software compilation, and LLM inference
